title: Boat Rental in Amalfi: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Planning to explore the Amalfi Coast by sea? Renting a boat from a marina like Marina di Praia offers a unique perspective, with prices starting from around €350 for a half-day motorboat. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators for a seamless experience.

Where to set off in Amalfi

Amalfi offers several departure points, each with distinct advantages. The main port is Marina di Amalfi, a busy harbour with ferry links and charter bases. Nearby, Marina di Praia provides a smaller, quieter slip for speedboats. Further along the coast, the port of Positano serves as a gateway to the Li Galli islands. Other real options include the harbour at Cetara, known for its fishing fleet, and the bay at Atrani, a short walk from Amalfi town. For longer charters, depart from Salerno's main marina, Molo Manfredi.

Which boat to rent

  1. Open motorboat (6-8 persons), Best for couples or small groups exploring coves like Fiordo di Furore; from €350 half-day.
  2. RIB or tender (6-10 persons), Nimble for reaching secluded beaches; from €400 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (8-12 persons), Spacious for day trips to Capri; from €1,200 full-day.
  4. Crewed yacht (10-18 persons), Luxury with skipper and crew, ideal for sunset cruises; from €2,500 full-day.

For a specific model, visit the Limpopo charter page which lists a 28-foot Axopar with twin outboards, perfect for a day trip to Li Galli.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Amalfi?

Costs vary by boat type, season, and duration, with half-day motorboats from €350. Here is a typical price band breakdown:

  • Shared group tour (3-4 hours): €80, €120 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €350, €600.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €1,200, €2,500.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (8 hours): €2,500, €5,000.

For an up-to-date selection, browse day charters in Amalfi on BoatHire24 to compare verified listings.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Amalfi?

In Italy, a licence is required for private boats over 30kW (about 40hp); operators confirm exact requirements. For smaller motorboats, many operators offer bareboat rentals without a licence. For larger vessels, a skipper is mandatory and often included. The peak season for boating in Amalfi runs from June to September, when the ‘Scirocco’ south-easterly wind can bring haze but also calm mornings. For a local insight, here is a tip from a skipper based in Marina di Amalfi:

“Hire a boat early in the morning, around 8 am, to avoid the afternoon crowds and the afternoon breeze that picks up around 2 pm.”

At a glance

  • Price range: €350, €5,000 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half-day) to 8 hours (full-day)
  • Capacity: 6 to 18 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Amalfi

Can I rent a boat in Amalfi without a licence?

Yes, many small motorboats under 40hp are available without a licence; the operator will confirm requirements at booking.

What is the best time of year for a boat rental in Amalfi?

The best time is between May and October, with July and August offering the warmest sea temperatures.

How long does it take to sail from Amalfi to Capri by boat?

The trip to Capri covers about 17 nautical miles and takes roughly 1 hour in a motorboat, depending on sea conditions.

Is it safe to anchor along the Amalfi Coast?

Many bays are safe to anchor in calm weather, but avoid areas marked as prohibited or near protected marine zones.

Whether you choose a self-drive motorboat or a crewed yacht, Amalfi offers a coastline best explored by sea. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Amalfi and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
